qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via workflow import from URL endpoint allows internal network access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns /rest/workflows/from-url using axios.get() on an attacker-supplied URL without SSRF protection, while the advisory concerns prototype pollution in the n8n XML node leading to RCE.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described XML node prototype-pollution root cause or an equivalent XML-node source-to-sink/control-flow condition.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-hqr4-h3xv-9m3r describes authenticated workflow editors achieving global prototype pollution via the n8n XML node, leading to RCE when combined with other nodes.
The session input manifest lists only one final qualifying finding: ssrf-workflow-import-from-url.

The qualifying SSRF finding targets GET /rest/workflows/from-url and raw axios.get(query.url) without SSRF protection, which is unrelated to XML node parsing, prototype pollution keys, or RCE through polluted Object prototypes.
